headline: USA open their home World Cup with a 4-1 statement — Balogun's brace answers the striker question
summary: On opening night in Los Angeles, the USMNT thumped Paraguay 4-1 — their first multi-goal World Cup win in 24 years. Folarin Balogun, long the open question at center-forward, scored a first-half brace (the second a left-footed top-corner finish) as the US led 3-0 by the break. Christian Pulisic ran the show before being rested at halftime. A dream start for Mauricio Pochettino's host nation in his first World Cup match in charge.
angles: Balogun's coming-of-age: the striker nobody trusted scores twice on the biggest stage — the USA's No. 9 question, answered
Pochettino's statement start: first multi-goal World Cup win in 24 years on home soil
Pulisic the architect: ran the first half, then got rested with the game already won — a luxury the US has never had
Host-nation dream night in LA: 3-0 by halftime, the building rocking
How far can THIS USA go? A demolition in game one resets expectations for the home World Cup
